---
pagetitle: "Qingyang Liu | Theoretical Physics"
description: "Academic homepage of Qingyang Liu, with research interests in lattice QCD, hadron spectroscopy, and relativistic potential models."
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::::: {.home-shell}

:::: {.home-hero}
<p class="hero-kicker">Theoretical Physics</p>

# Qingyang Liu {.hero-title}

<p class="hero-subtitle">Theoretical Physics <span aria-hidden="true">·</span> Lattice QCD <span aria-hidden="true">·</span> Hadron Spectroscopy</p>

<p class="hero-intro">I study the structure and interactions of hadrons using lattice field theory and relativistic models. My current interests center on charmonium and charmoniumlike states, two-hadron systems, and the connection between correlation functions and spatial observables.</p>

<p class="hero-actions"><a class="btn btn-primary" href="research.qmd">Explore research</a> <a class="btn btn-outline-primary" href="publications.qmd">View publications</a></p>
::::

## Research Interests {.home-section-title}

<p class="section-intro">Questions at the intersection of nonperturbative QCD, hadron structure, and controlled phenomenological modeling.</p>

:::: {.card-grid .card-grid--three}
::: {.academic-card}
### Lattice QCD

Correlation functions and spatial observables for nonperturbative studies of hadrons.
:::

::: {.academic-card}
### Hadron spectroscopy

The spectrum and internal organization of charmonium and charmoniumlike states.
:::

::: {.academic-card}
### Hadron interactions

Two-hadron systems, four-point functions, and their spatial and dynamical content.
:::

::: {.academic-card}
### Charm physics

Heavy-quark systems as a setting for connecting QCD calculations with effective descriptions.
:::

::: {.academic-card}
### Relativistic models

Cornell-type interactions and Dirac formulations for complementary theoretical analysis.
:::
::::

## Selected Projects {.home-section-title}

:::: {.card-grid .card-grid--three}
::: {.project-card}
<p class="card-label">Lattice structure</p>

### Portraits of charmoniumlike states

Studying spatial density correlations as probes of hadron structure in lattice QCD.

[Research context →](research.qmd#spatial-structures-of-charmoniumlike-states){.card-link}
:::

::: {.project-card}
<p class="card-label">Two-hadron systems</p>

### Four-point correlation analysis

Developing analysis strategies for spatially resolved two-hadron correlation functions.

[Research context →](research.qmd#two-hadron-systems-and-four-point-correlation-functions){.card-link}
:::

::: {.project-card}
<p class="card-label">Relativistic modeling</p>

### Cornell–Dirac models

Exploring relativistic potential descriptions of heavy-quark systems and their wave functions.

[Research context →](research.qmd#cornelldirac-models){.card-link}
:::
::::

## Recent Work {.home-section-title}

:::: {.recent-work}
<p class="card-label">Manuscript</p>

### Portraits of Charmoniumlike States

Qingyang Liu, Xiangyu Jiang, Ying Chen, Chunjiang Shi, and Wei Sun

The manuscript develops a spatial perspective on charmoniumlike states. Bibliographic links will be added when verified public records are available.

[Publication details →](publications.qmd){.card-link}
::::

:::::
